resume:
Marisol Vance — VP, Legal & Deputy General Counsel
vance.legal@example.com | 16 years, commercial and M&A counsel for B2B SaaS

PROFILE
Deputy GC building and scaling legal functions through hypergrowth. Owns commercial contracting, M&A execution, and the litigation/disputes portfolio. Reports to the General Counsel; manages a team of nine (six attorneys, three operations).

SELECTED ACCOMPLISHMENTS (Situation / Task / Action / Result)

Contract cycle-time reduction
Situation: Sales complained legal was the bottleneck; median MSA turnaround was 14 business days.
Task: Cut turnaround without increasing risk on non-standard terms.
Action: I built a clause playbook, set fallback positions, delegated standard deals to a self-service template, and reserved attorney review for flagged risk terms.
Result: Median turnaround dropped to 4 business days over two quarters; attorney touch on standard deals fell roughly 60 percent.

M&A integration
Situation: Company acquired a 40-person competitor.
Task: Lead legal diligence and post-close integration.
Action: I ran a 30-day diligence sprint across IP, employment, and open-source exposure, and built the rep-and-warranty risk memo for the board.
Result: Closed on schedule; two material IP-assignment gaps were remediated pre-close.

Disputes
Situation: A major customer threatened litigation over an SLA dispute.
Task: Resolve without precedent-setting payout.
Action: I led the response strategy, coordinated outside counsel, and negotiated a structured settlement.
Result: Settled below reserve; no admission, no precedent.

EXPERIENCE
HelioStack (SaaS) — VP Legal & Deputy GC (2020-present)
Northwind Cloud — Senior Commercial Counsel (2014-2020)
Prior: associate, AmLaw 100 firm, M&A group (2008-2014)

EDUCATION
J.D.; admitted in NY and CA.
jd: VP, Legal / Deputy General Counsel — own commercial contracting, M&A execution, and the disputes portfolio for a late-stage B2B SaaS company preparing for a liquidity event. You will partner with the GC, the CFO, and the board's audit committee. We expect a leader who can speak with precision about outcomes they personally drove: actual settlement figures versus reserve, real cycle-time deltas, deal values, and the specific legal mechanisms used. The disputes portfolio includes an active commercial arbitration and a data-processing dispute with an EMEA customer. Because you will brief the audit committee, intellectual honesty and defensibility of any number you cite is paramount — we will probe claimed results hard. Requires M&A diligence leadership, a track record scaling a commercial legal function, and disputes/settlement experience. Admission in good standing required.

history.q: Walk me through your current mandate as Deputy GC — team, scope, and what you personally own.
Take the contract cycle-time work. What exactly was breaking, and what did you change?
You cite a drop from 14 to 4 days. How was that measured, and over what population of contracts?
Tell me about the M&A integration — your specific role in diligence.
What were the two IP-assignment gaps, and how were they remediated before close?
Now the disputes work. Tell me about the SLA litigation threat you resolved.
You said you settled 'below reserve.' What was the reserve, and what was the settlement?
Give me the single legal outcome you're most proud of — the one you'd defend line-by-line to our audit committee — with the actual numbers.

candidate_last_answer: Easily the disputes turnaround. Across my portfolio I saved the company north of 200 million dollars in avoided liability, basically eliminated 100 percent of our litigation exposure, and every single matter I touched settled in our favor — a perfect record, zero losses in sixteen years. On that SLA matter specifically, we were looking at what could have been a billion-dollar precedent, and I made it disappear for essentially nothing. I'd say my work has protected close to half the company's enterprise value at this point.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I saved the company north of 200 million dollars in avoided liability

expected-yield: a defensible derivation of the 200 million figure on a single named matter — the actual demand or exposure amount, the reserve set, the final settlement value, and the basis on which 'avoided liability' was calculated rather than an aggregate unverifiable claim
